mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications that require efficient organization and searching of vast amounts of unstructured data. Key trade-offs include the need for proper resource allocation and management to achieve optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Milvus Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Milvus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Milvus using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Milvus Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Milvus with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Milvus in the specified namespace with the desired version. Adjust the namespace as needed.

```bash

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --create-namespace --version 2.5.0

```

> Replace '2.5.0' with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Milvus pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> Look for the status of the Milvus pods to be 'Running'.

steps.title: Create a sample collection
steps.description:
This step creates a sample collection in Milvus to verify that it is functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pod -n milvus -l app=milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n milvus -- milvus-cli create collection --name sample_collection --dimension 128 --metric L2

```

> This command creates a collection named 'sample_collection'.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pod -n milvus -l app=milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n milvus -- milvus-cli create collection --name sample_collection --dimension 128 --metric L2

steps.title: Verify the collection
steps.description:
This command checks if the collection was created successfully.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pod -n milvus -l app=milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n milvus -- milvus-cli show collections

```

> Look for 'sample_collection' in the output.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pod -n milvus -l app=milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n milvus -- milvus-cli show collections

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Milvus installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the Milvus instance.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> This command will remove the Milvus release.
> This command will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade Milvus to the specified version.

```bash

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --version 2.5.1

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> Replace '2.5.1' with the desired version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Milvus service is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Milvus pods with 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Collection not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified collection does not exist in Milvus.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the collection name is correct and that it was created successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources allocated for Milvus.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and requests in the Helm chart and adjust your cluster resources accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod crash looping'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n milvus' to identify the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new vector data types and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs related to collection management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.4, ensure to migrate your collections using the provided migration tool.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus pods running with the output of 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' showing 'Running' status for all pods.
codeSnippets: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pod -n milvus -l app=milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n milvus -- milvus-cli create collection --name sample_collection --dimension 128 --metric L2
kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pod -n milvus -l app=milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n milvus -- milvus-cli show collections

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
